message:
fix(fr-command): BITPOS short-circuits on missing key before parsing trailing args [bd-frankenredis-4urm]

Upstream t_string.c::bitposCommand looks up the key BEFORE parsing
the trailing [start [end [BIT|BYTE]]] args. A missing key
short-circuits to (bit ? -1 : 0) regardless of any unknown trailing
arguments.

fr-command parsed the args first via a strict argc<3/argc>6 gate,
returning 'ERR syntax error' for cases like 'BITPOS nokey 1 0 -1
NOSUCH' even though the key didn't exist.

Reorder bitpos to (1) parse the bit arg, (2) check key_type —
None → return Integer(if bit==1 {-1} else {0}); wrong type →
WrongType — (3) parse trailing args. Update the
bitpos_too_many_arguments_is_syntax_error test to SET a key first
so it actually exercises the syntax-error branch (which now fires
only when the key is present, matching upstream).
